author: u/truestking99
title: Made a cake for a girl and got stood up and ghosted
body: One of my prompts on Bumble is me talking about why I bake — specifically tres leches cakes of different flavors. This girl commented on that, so I suggested we do a baking exchange.
Time and date were set up. Earlier in the day I checked the app and we were still matched.
I was about to get ready, so I checked again because I wanted to show her the cake before. Bang — unmatched. An hour before the time.
The cake is carrot cake tres leches. I don't even want it.
Now I have to wash a bunch of dishes. I just wanted a cute girl to eat my food. Now I'm depressed.
narration:
So I'm on Bumble. And one of my prompts on the app is about why I bake. Specifically tres leches cake. Different flavors. It's kind of my thing.

This girl matches with me. She specifically COMMENTS on the baking thing. I'm thinking — bingo. I have an in.

So I suggest a baking exchange. We pick a date. We pick a time. We're locked in.

The day arrives. I'm in the kitchen. CARROT CAKE tres leches. I'm putting in WORK. Flour. Eggs. Three kinds of milk. Hours of prep. The whole production.

Cake is done. It's GORGEOUS. I want to send her a photo. Just like — surprise, look what I made.

Unmatched. ONE HOUR before our scheduled meet-up. Just gone.

I'm standing in my kitchen. Apron on. With this beautiful carrot cake tres leches. That nobody is going to eat. With me.

And I now have to wash. Every. Single. Dish. Alone.

I just wanted a cute girl to eat my food.

Now I'm just depressed. Eating cake. By myself. Like I'm in a Hallmark movie. About loneliness.
